title: SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES - 211info
text: OREGON COAST COMMUNITY ACTION COOS SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers housing-focused case management, referrals to community services, limited short-term rental or deposit assistance, and housing placement support. All assistance is time-limited and based on program guidelines. 1855 Thomas Avenue Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Veteran households may be considered for SSVF if they: -Include at least one eligible veteran (per VA guidelines) -Are homeless or at risk of homelessness -Have a household income at or below 80% of Area Median Income (AMI) for Coos County -Meet additional SSVF and funding requirements Meeting eligibility criteria does not guarantee financial assistance. Hours: English, Spanish interpretation by arrangement Email (541) 435-7080 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (541) 435-7080 Main phone (541) 435-7754 Main phone South Coast Food Share (541) 888-3717 Administration South Coast Head Start Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website or walk in to complete a Housing Stabilization Services Application. All veterans must apply through ORCCA's General Housing Services first. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: DD214, photo ID, Social Security card, proof of income LANGUAGES: English, Spanish interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Coos County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Located in the same complex as Coos Health and Wellness and Advanced Health. Services: Case/Care Management * Homeless Veterans Case/Care Management * Veterans Homeless Permanent Supportive Housing * Veterans Housing Related Coordinated Entry * Veterans Rent Payment Assistance * Homeless Veterans Rent Payment Assistance * Veterans Rental Deposit Assistance * Homeless Veterans Rental Deposit Assistance * Veterans Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS/SCHOOLS - EARLY HEAD START EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS/SCHOOLS - HEAD START HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - MEDNET HOUSING/SHELTER - HOUSING STABILIZATION SERVICES HOUSING/SHELTER - WEATHERIZATION ASSISTANCE HUMAN REPRODUCTION - FAMILY SUPPORT AND CONNECTIONS JUDICIAL SERVICES - COURT APPOINTED SPECIAL ADVOCATES UTILITIES - UTILITY ASSISTANCE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. OREGON COAST COMMUNITY ACTION COOS
